methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $250.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $10.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $5.6B

private.name: Modal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $1.1B

data.narrative:
xAI, a Series E AI & Software company, is valued at $250B post-money following $42B in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises one public comparable (Palantir) and eight private AI & Software firms at similar stages, including Crusoe Energy ($10.0B), SandboxAQ ($5.6B), Ayar Labs ($3.8B), WEKA ($1.6B), and Modal ($1.1B).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement to the last post-money valuation of $250B, implying $350.0B. Peer-median methodology anchors to the median valuation of five same-sector, same-stage comparables: $3.8B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $10.0B. The substantial divergence reflects the outlier scale of xAI relative to its peer set.

Triangulation across both methods yields a range of $3.2B (low) to $402.5B (high), with a median of $176.9B. The wide spread reflects fundamental uncertainty about whether xAI merits valuation parity with early-stage AI infrastructure peers or should trade at a material premium consistent with its capital raise size and founder profile. The peer-median approach suggests xAI trades at a 66x multiple of comparable Series E valuations—a gap that demands explicit conviction on differentiated technology, market position, or capital intensity relative to the reference set.
